items.title: Woman, 20, dead after being struck by vehicle while crossing road near Mississauga hospital, say Peel police
Mississauga: Gateway to the best of Ontario
Driver arrested, female pedestrian dead after being struck by vehicle in Mississauga
items.summary: A woman in her 20s has died after being struck by a driver in a vehicle in Mississauga, according to Peel police.

items.summary: Peel police have identified two teenage brothers as suspects in a fatal shooting in Brampton last week.

items.title: Severe Thunderstorm issued for Mississauga; damaging winds and heavy rain possible today
items.summary: Storm conditions are expected to continue through the afternoon across much of southern Ontario. Given the localized nature of thunderstorms, exact impacts and locations can be difficult to predict until storms develop and move into the area. Storm conditions are expected to begin around noon today, with the most significant impacts anticipated through the early afternoon. These thunderstorms are expected across much of southern Ontario. This storm brings a risk of damaging wind gusts and the potential for heavy rain and large hail.

items.title: City outdoor pools and recreation programs resume normal operations as air quality continues to improve
items.summary: Due to wildfires in other parts of Ontario, Mississauga experienced poor air quality and a Very High (10+) Air Quality Health Index (AQHI) last week. As a result, to support public health and safety, outdoor pools, and Mississauga Celebration Square wading pool were closed and some recreation programs were adjusted. Based on reports from Environment and Climate Change Canada (ECCC), air quality is expected to continue improving through the day.
